dev_updates.title: Announcing Early Access Roadmap and Community Hub
dev_updates.summary: Overview of our early access plans, roadmap structure, and how you can participate through the new community hub.
dev_updates.body:
Today we are unveiling our early access roadmap and the community hub that powers this very site. The roadmap will show upcoming features across combat, co-op, crafting, and performance-related improvements. Each card on the roadmap can be filtered by platform, release window, and status, giving you a clear view of what is coming next.

The community hub is where you can submit feedback, report bugs, and participate in discussions and polls. Your input will directly influence future roadmap items, so we encourage you to bookmark features, upvote suggestions, and share your thoughts regularly.

dev_updates.title: Alpha Combat Update: New Abilities and Enemy Behaviors
dev_updates.summary: Details on the first major combat update in alpha, including new abilities, smarter enemies, and roadmap notes.
dev_updates.body:
Our first alpha combat update introduces several new player abilities and significantly improved enemy behaviors. You will notice enemies coordinating attacks, reacting to your defensive choices, and using the environment more intelligently. These changes lay the foundation for late-game combat encounters and more challenging boss fights.

We have also added new combat-related entries to the roadmap, including advanced dodging options and weapon-specific combo trees. Please visit the Roadmap page to see what is planned for 2025 and let us know what you think in the Community feedback section.

dev_updates.title: Crafting System Deep Dive and Upcoming Changes
dev_updates.summary: A look at how crafting works under the hood and what improvements are planned based on your feedback.
dev_updates.body:
Crafting has been one of the most discussed systems in our community, and we have been listening closely to your feedback. In this deep dive, we explain how resource quality affects outcomes, how blueprint rarity ties into late-game progression, and what we are doing to reduce unnecessary grind.

We are planning several roadmap items that expand crafting tags, add new workstation types, and introduce cooperative crafting bonuses for parties. Keep an eye on the Roadmap page under the Crafting category, and join the Community discussions to share your favorite build recipes and suggestions.

dev_updates.title: Performance Optimization Roadmap for PC and Consoles
dev_updates.summary: Outlining our performance optimization goals across PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S for 2025.
dev_updates.body:
We want to give you a clear picture of our performance goals for the year. This performance optimization roadmap focuses on improving frame pacing, reducing hitching during streaming, and lowering CPU spikes during large-scale combat encounters. On PC, we are targeting better utilization of multi-core CPUs and more consistent behavior across a wide range of GPUs.

On consoles, our priority is stable frame rates in both Quality and Performance modes, with special attention to PS5 and Xbox Series X|S. Several new roadmap entries tagged with 'performance' have been added for 2025. You can filter the Roadmap by the performance tag and by platform to see when specific optimizations are scheduled to land.

dev_updates.title: First Look: Co-op Gameplay Trailer and Feature Highlights
dev_updates.summary: Watch the first co-op gameplay trailer and learn what to expect from shared progression and matchmaking.
dev_updates.body:
Our first co-op gameplay trailer is live, showcasing four-player dungeon runs, shared loot rules, and synchronized boss mechanics. The trailer highlights how different roles can combine abilities to create powerful synergies, especially in late-game challenge modes.

We have also updated the Roadmap with new co-op features across PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S. These entries include improved matchmaking, cross-platform invites, and special co-op-only events arriving later in 2025.

dev_updates.title: Xbox Series X|S Technical Preview Details
dev_updates.summary: Information on how to join the Xbox Series X|S technical preview and what features are included.
dev_updates.body:
We are opening registrations for the Xbox Series X|S technical preview, which will focus on validating platform-specific features such as Quick Resume support, HDR tuning, and controller-centric UI adjustments. Participants will gain early access to a subset of regions, core campaign missions, and select co-op experiences.

The Roadmap now includes an 'Under Review' cluster of Xbox-specific items based on feedback from our console players. As you participate, please use the Community bug reports section to log issues and help us refine the experience.

dev_updates.title: Cross-Play Beta and Account Linking
dev_updates.summary: Cross-play beta opens with support for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S, along with new account linking options.
dev_updates.body:
The cross-play beta is now live, enabling players on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X|S to team up in most co-op activities. During the beta, you will be able to form parties across platforms, share progression for core campaign milestones, and test our new cross-platform friends list.

We have added several new Roadmap items tagged as 'co-op' and 'cross-play' to track follow-up work, including improved party stability and better error messaging. Please visit the Community feedback section to tell us how cross-play feels so far.

dev_updates.title: Holiday Patch 0.8.9: Events, Rewards, and Stability Fixes
dev_updates.summary: Patch notes for the holiday update introducing limited-time events, reward tracks, and several stability improvements.
dev_updates.body:
Patch 0.8.9 ushers in our first holiday event, complete with themed quests, cosmetic rewards, and a new limited-time co-op challenge. We have also fixed multiple crashes related to loading into crowded hubs and switching rapidly between regions.

While this patch is not a dedicated performance update, you may notice smoother loading and fewer hitches when joining co-op sessions. A full performance-focused pass is still planned for early 2026, as described in our performance optimization roadmap.

dev_updates.title: Hotfix 0.9.3: Stability and Performance Improvements
dev_updates.summary: A small hotfix addressing crashes and incremental performance improvements ahead of larger updates.
dev_updates.body:
Hotfix 0.9.3 targets several crash signatures reported through the Community bug reports section, particularly on PS5 and PC. We have reduced instances of crashes when alt-tabbing on PC and improved error handling when matchmaking fails.

This hotfix also includes incremental performance improvements, such as better streaming behavior in dense city hubs and reduced CPU spikes during large combat encounters. These changes are an early step toward our broader performance goals outlined in the Performance Optimization Roadmap.

dev_updates.title: Design Blog: Late-Game Weapon Balance Philosophy
dev_updates.summary: Explaining how we approach late-game weapon damage, scaling, and counterplay.
dev_updates.body:
In this design blog, we outline our philosophy for late-game weapon balance. Many of you have shared feedback that some weapons feel disproportionately strong or weak once you reach the highest difficulty tiers. We agree that clarity around scaling, armor interactions, and status effects could be much better.

Our upcoming roadmap items in the Gameplay Balance category will adjust weapon damage curves, improve tooltips, and make enemy resistances more readable. We encourage you to continue posting detailed feedback in the Community section, especially around specific builds that feel out of line.

dev_updates.title: Co-op Systems Update: Matchmaking, Roles, and Rewards
dev_updates.summary: A status update on co-op systems, including matchmaking improvements and role-based rewards.
dev_updates.body:
We have been closely monitoring co-op participation rates and reading your feedback on matchmaking and rewards. In this update, we explain how our new role-based matchmaking aims to create more balanced parties and how we are tweaking reward tables to better recognize support and control playstyles.

Several new co-op roadmap items for spring 2026 have been created, particularly for PC and PS5. These include improved lobby tools, better reconnection flows, and more transparent difficulty indicators when joining higher-tier content.

dev_updates.title: Performance Deep Dive: Next-Gen Consoles and High-End PC
dev_updates.summary: The newest performance update covering frame rate targets and optimization strategies for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and high-end PCs.
dev_updates.body:
This performance deep dive is our most detailed look yet at how the game runs on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and high-end PC configurations. We share internal benchmarks, explain our frame rate targets for each performance mode, and outline how dynamic resolution and streaming budgets are tuned differently per platform.

We also discuss ongoing performance work scheduled on the Roadmap for spring 2026, including CPU optimizations for large AI encounters and GPU improvements for our most complex environments. If you are interested in performance and wish to follow our progress, bookmark the relevant roadmap entries tagged 'performance' and share your hardware specs and experiences in the Community.

dev_updates.title: Patch 0.9.5: New Region, Enemies, and Co-op Challenges
dev_updates.summary: Patch notes for 0.9.5 introducing a new region, enemy types, and additional co-op challenge tiers.
dev_updates.body:
Patch 0.9.5 unlocks the Ashen Frontier region, featuring new enemy families, environmental hazards, and secrets tailored for both solo and co-op play. We have added fresh enemy archetypes that lean into crowd control and positioning, encouraging creative team compositions.

This update also adds new co-op challenge tiers with better rewards, alongside a number of quality-of-life improvements to the map and party UI. A separate performance-focused patch will follow later, so this update does not include major performance changes beyond minor stability tweaks.

dev_updates.title: Bugfix Roundup: Top Community-Reported Issues Addressed
dev_updates.summary: A quick overview of the most impactful community-reported bugs we have fixed this month.
dev_updates.body:
Thanks to your detailed bug reports, we have resolved several high-impact issues across all platforms. This includes multiple crash fixes on PS5 related to long play sessions, a memory leak on Xbox Series X|S when frequently changing regions, and several quest blockers in the new Ashen Frontier region.

We encourage you to continue using the Bug Reports tab in the Community to log new issues, especially if you encounter crashes, progression blockers, or unexpectedly low performance on specific hardware. Your reports directly shape our priorities for future hotfixes and patches.

dev_updates.title: Preview: Spring 2026 Features for PC and PS5
dev_updates.summary: A preview of planned spring 2026 features focused on PC and PS5, including combat, co-op, and quality-of-life updates.
dev_updates.body:
As we move into spring 2026, we want to highlight some of the key features planned for PC and PS5. These include new combat encounters tuned for higher difficulties, additional co-op challenge tiers, and roadmap items focused on improving UI clarity and matchmaking flows.

Most of these features are already visible on the Roadmap with a status of 'planned' and release dates between March and June 2026. Be sure to filter by platform and status, then sort by upvotes if you want to see which upcoming features the community is most excited about.

poll_options.text: More resource variety and rare materials
Blueprint discovery and clearer unlock paths
Reduce crafting grind and time gating
Xbox Series X|S performance and stability

polls.question: Which crafting improvements should we prioritize for the next major patch?
polls.description: Vote on which area of the crafting system you want us to focus on first. Your choice will influence the next balance and UX pass.

polls.question: Which platform should be our top performance optimization priority for early 2026?
polls.description: We are planning a series of performance-focused updates. Let us know where you most want to see improvements.

polls.question: What type of endgame activity do you enjoy the most?
polls.description: Help us decide where to invest more design time for upcoming endgame features and rewards.

polls.question: For the next update, should we focus more on bug fixes or new content?
polls.description: We are planning our next quarterly update and want your input on how to balance bug fixing against adding new features and regions.

roadmap_features.title: Improved PC Co-op Matchmaking and Role Preferences
roadmap_features.short_summary: Revamped co-op matchmaking flow with role preferences and better connection quality checks on PC.
roadmap_features.description: This feature overhauls the co-op matchmaking experience on PC. Players will be able to set preferred roles, minimum gear score, and language preferences when queuing for dungeons and raids. The system will prioritize lower-latency lobbies and avoid repeatedly matching you with groups you have recently left. Cross-play rules are also being refined so PC players can opt into or out of mixed-platform matchmaking.

roadmap_features.title: Cross-Character Shared Co-op Progression on PC
roadmap_features.short_summary: Allow co-op unlocks and dungeon checkpoints to be shared across all characters on a PC account.
roadmap_features.description: Many players have requested that co-op milestones carry across alts. This feature introduces account-wide co-op progression for PC, including unlocked dungeon tiers, completed raid wings, and certain cosmetic rewards. The system will track which milestones are shareable and clearly indicate what is bound to a character. It also lays groundwork for future cross-platform progression.

roadmap_features.title: PC Co-op Raid Finder and Flexible Queue Sizes
roadmap_features.short_summary: Introduce a raid finder tool on PC with support for flexible group sizes and role requirements.
roadmap_features.description: The raid finder will allow PC players to queue solo or in small groups and be matched into full raid parties. Leaders can set minimum requirements, such as completed story chapters or target item levels. The feature includes automatic backfill for players who disconnect and clearer explanations of raid mechanics in the pre-queue screen.

roadmap_features.title: Dynamic Co-op Difficulty Scaling for PC Parties
roadmap_features.short_summary: Tune enemy health, damage, and rewards dynamically based on party size and average power on PC.
roadmap_features.description: Co-op content can feel either trivial or punishing depending on your party composition. This feature introduces dynamic difficulty scaling for PC parties in dungeons and open-world events. Enemy stats, density, and reward quality will adjust based on party size and average gear score. Clear UI indicators will show the current difficulty tier so players understand what to expect.

roadmap_features.title: Late-Game Weapon Damage Tuning and Scaling Pass
roadmap_features.short_summary: Rebalance late-game weapon damage curves, armor interactions, and status effects.
roadmap_features.description: This combat update focuses on late-game weapon balance across all archetypes. We are revisiting damage curves, critical scaling, armor penetration rules, and how status effects stack in high-tier content. The goal is to reduce outlier builds that one-shot enemies while bringing underperforming weapons up to par. Tooltips will be updated to better explain how damage is calculated, and training dummies in the capital will show more detailed breakdowns.

roadmap_features.title: Boss AI Overhaul and New Attack Patterns
roadmap_features.short_summary: Make boss encounters more reactive and less predictable, especially on higher difficulties.
roadmap_features.description: Bosses will gain new attack patterns, improved target selection, and more visible telegraphs for lethal abilities. On higher difficulty tiers, certain bosses will chain abilities together in new ways and adapt to repeated player strategies. This update also adds optional challenge modifiers for players who want tougher fights with better rewards.

roadmap_features.title: Perfect Block and Counterattack Mechanics
roadmap_features.short_summary: Introduce a new timing-based block that rewards skillful play with powerful counterattacks.
roadmap_features.description: The new perfect block mechanic allows players to negate most incoming damage if they time their block within a tight window. Successful perfect blocks will generate a resource that can be spent on powerful counterattacks or defensive buffs. This update includes new animations, audio cues, and tutorial tips to help players learn the timing.

roadmap_features.title: Elemental Status Effect Rework
roadmap_features.short_summary: Rework burning, freezing, shocking, and poisoning effects to scale more cleanly into late game.
roadmap_features.description: Elemental status effects will be rebalanced to provide clearer roles: burning for sustained damage, freezing for control, shocking for burst, and poisoning for hybrid utility. The rework ensures these effects scale logically with weapon power and enemy resistances throughout 2025 endgame content. New UI icons and debuff timers will help players understand what is active at a glance.

roadmap_features.title: Advanced Controller Remapping on Xbox Series X|S
roadmap_features.short_summary: Expanded controller remapping options and presets tailored to Xbox Series X|S players.
roadmap_features.description: This feature would add more granular controller remapping for Xbox Series X|S, including per-action binding, hold/toggle options, and sensitivity presets. Accessibility-conscious presets would be available for players who prefer one-handed controls or simplified inputs. We are collecting feedback on which actions are hardest to reach on the current default layout.

roadmap_features.title: Quick Resume Stability Improvements on Xbox Series X|S
roadmap_features.short_summary: Investigate and fix issues when returning to the game via Quick Resume.
roadmap_features.description: Xbox Series X|S players have reported long load times and occasional crashes when using Quick Resume. This potential feature focuses on improving reconnection to online services, refreshing co-op lobbies, and ensuring save data integrity after resuming. We are analyzing telemetry and community bug reports to determine the scope of necessary changes.

roadmap_features.title: Crash on Region Change After Xbox Resume
roadmap_features.short_summary: Address specific crash signatures occurring shortly after resuming and changing regions on Xbox Series X|S.
roadmap_features.description: Some Xbox Series X|S players experience crashes when resuming the game and quickly fast traveling or changing regions. This under-review feature tracks a potential fix that restructures how assets are streamed and validated after resume. If implemented, it should significantly reduce these crash signatures and improve overall stability.

roadmap_features.title: In-Game Co-op Voice Chat Improvements on Xbox Series X|S
roadmap_features.short_summary: Improve audio quality, push-to-talk options, and mute controls for Xbox co-op voice chat.
roadmap_features.description: We have received feedback that the in-game co-op voice chat on Xbox Series X|S can be unreliable and lacks some expected controls. This feature would add better noise suppression, optional push-to-talk, and clearer per-player mute and volume options. Integration with the system-level Xbox party interface is also being evaluated.

roadmap_features.title: Achievement Sync Reliability on Xbox Series X|S
roadmap_features.short_summary: Ensure achievements unlock and sync correctly between offline and online sessions on Xbox.
roadmap_features.description: Some Xbox Series X|S players have reported achievements failing to unlock or sync after playing offline. This under-review feature would adjust how progress is tracked locally and reconciled with Xbox Live once you reconnect. We are monitoring community reports to validate reproduction steps and potential edge cases.

roadmap_features.title: Spring 2026 Performance Pass for PC and PS5
roadmap_features.short_summary: CPU and GPU optimizations aimed at smoother frame rates in dense combat and city hubs on PC and PS5.
roadmap_features.description: This performance-focused update targets CPU spikes during large AI encounters and GPU bottlenecks in the most complex city hubs. On PC, we are improving multi-threaded job scheduling and asset streaming on high-end and mid-range configurations. On PS5, we are optimizing our performance mode to reduce frame-time variance while maintaining image quality.

roadmap_features.title: In-Game LFG Board for PC and PS5 Co-op
roadmap_features.short_summary: Add a persistent in-game looking-for-group board for dungeons, raids, and events on PC and PS5.
roadmap_features.description: The new LFG board will allow PC and PS5 players to post and browse group listings without sitting in a matchmaking queue. Listings can specify activity type, preferred roles, language, and voice chat requirements. This tool is designed to complement automated matchmaking, giving players more control over who they team up with.

roadmap_features.title: New Tiered Endgame Dungeons for PC and PS5
roadmap_features.short_summary: Introduce a set of tiered endgame dungeons tuned for coordinated groups on PC and PS5.
roadmap_features.description: This feature adds several new endgame dungeons with scalable difficulty tiers and rotating weekly modifiers. Encounters are designed to reward coordinated play, tight execution, and creative build combinations. Progress will be tracked on a per-account basis so players can chase seasonal titles and cosmetics.

roadmap_features.title: UI and Accessibility Overhaul for PC and PS5
roadmap_features.short_summary: Improve readability, font scaling, colorblind modes, and input hints across PC and PS5.
roadmap_features.description: This overhaul focuses on making the UI more accessible and readable on a wide range of screens. Players on PC and PS5 will gain finer control over font scaling, HUD clutter, and colorblind-safe palettes. Contextual input hints will better differentiate between keyboard/mouse and controller prompts, reducing confusion when switching devices.

roadmap_features.title: Cinematic Photo Mode for PC and PS5
roadmap_features.short_summary: Add a robust photo mode with filters, depth-of-field, and character posing tools.
roadmap_features.description: Players on PC and PS5 will be able to pause the action and capture cinematic screenshots using a new photo mode. Features include free camera movement within a safe radius, multiple filters, depth-of-field controls, and character poses. A spoiler-safe option will automatically hide certain story elements when enabled.

roadmap_features.title: PC Mod Support Beta
roadmap_features.short_summary: Limited beta for curated PC mod support, starting with cosmetic and UI-only mods.
roadmap_features.description: This feature introduces a curated mod support beta on PC, focusing initially on cosmetic and UI-only modifications. Players will be able to browse and enable approved mods from an in-game menu. The beta will help us evaluate performance, security, and support implications before expanding to deeper gameplay-affecting mods.

community_posts.title: Best early-game crafting routes after patch 0.9.5
community_posts.body: With the Ashen Frontier and new materials in patch 0.9.5, my old crafting routes feel outdated. Where are you all farming your early-game mats now, and which stations do you prioritize unlocking first? Share your favorite routes and any tips for minimizing travel time between nodes.

community_posts.title: Is crafting too grindy compared to just chasing drops?
community_posts.body: I feel like I spend more time collecting materials and waiting on crafting timers than actually playing content. For those of you who focus on crafting, does it feel rewarding enough compared to simply farming drops? What changes would make the crafting loop feel less grindy without trivializing gear progression?

community_posts.title: Share your favorite late-game crafting-focused builds
community_posts.body: For players who have pushed into late-game content, which builds really shine when you lean into crafting bonuses and specialized gear? I am curious how people are using crafted affixes, set bonuses, and consumables to support high-tier dungeon runs. Screenshots or detailed breakdowns of your crafting setups are very welcome.

community_posts.title: PS5 crash when fast traveling from Ashen Frontier hub
community_posts.body: On PS5, my game consistently crashes to the console dashboard when I fast travel from the Ashen Frontier hub back to the capital city. It usually happens within 3–5 seconds of confirming the fast travel. I have rebuilt my database and checked for corrupted saves, but the issue keeps coming back.

community_posts.title: PS5 hard crash after 3+ hour sessions
community_posts.body: I am getting a hard crash on PS5 after playing for around three hours straight. The game freezes for a second, then the console reports an error and forces a restart. It seems to happen more often when I am in busy city hubs or joining co-op dungeons rapidly.

community_posts.title: PS5 crash on loading into co-op dungeon
community_posts.body: Whenever I queue for a co-op dungeon on PS5, there is a chance the game crashes as soon as the loading screen finishes. I usually hear the dungeon audio for a split second and then get kicked back to the PS5 error screen. It feels more frequent when matchmaking with cross-play turned on.

community_posts.title: PS5 crash when entering photo mode in crowded hubs
community_posts.body: I have had multiple crashes on PS5 when trying to enter photo mode while standing in the main city hub. The game stutters, the UI partially fades, and then the app closes with a generic error. It does not seem to happen in quieter areas or dungeons, only in heavily populated social spaces.

community_posts.title: Severe FPS drops in city hub on mid-range PC
community_posts.body: On my mid-range PC (RTX 3060 and Ryzen 5), the frame rate tanks to around 25–30 FPS whenever I enter the main city hub, even on medium settings. Other zones run at a stable 60 FPS. I have tried turning off motion blur and lowering crowd density, but the stutters still happen, especially near the market.

community_posts.title: Xbox Series X|S freeze after Quick Resume and region change
community_posts.body: On Xbox Series X, the game often freezes for 10–15 seconds after using Quick Resume and then fast traveling to another region. Sometimes it recovers, but other times it crashes back to the dashboard. This behavior started after the last patch and seems related to asset streaming.

community_posts.title: Cross-play invites failing between PC and PS5
community_posts.body: My friend on PS5 cannot accept my party invites from PC about half the time. The invite notification appears but fails with a generic error when they try to join. Disabling and re-enabling cross-play in the settings sometimes helps, but the issue always comes back within a few sessions.

community_posts.title: Elite enemies take wildly different damage from the same weapons
community_posts.body: In late-game dungeons, some elite enemies melt instantly while others feel like complete bullet sponges when hit with the exact same weapon and build. It makes it hard to understand whether I should be adjusting my gear or if their resistances are just tuned too high. Clearer rules or more consistent tuning for elite armor would really help.

community_posts.title: Support builds feel under-rewarded in co-op scoreboards
community_posts.body: Playing as a support feels fun mechanically, but the end-of-mission scoreboard mainly highlights damage and kills. Healers and crowd-control builds often look weak even when they carry difficult runs. It would be great to see metrics and rewards that recognize shielding, healing, debuffs, and clutch revives.

community_posts.title: Performance mode on PS5 has noticeable image flicker
community_posts.body: While performance mode on PS5 feels much smoother, there is a persistent image flicker around foliage and fine details when moving quickly. It can be distracting during fast-paced combat. I would love an option that slightly relaxes the frame rate target in exchange for more stable image quality.

community_posts.title: Material storage tab for crafting would be a huge quality-of-life improvement
community_posts.body: Right now my inventory is constantly clogged with crafting materials, and it is hard to keep track of what I actually need. A dedicated material storage tab that auto-sorts by rarity and type would make crafting sessions much smoother. It would also help when planning big crafting projects for late-game gear upgrades.

community_posts.title: Which difficulty did you pick for your first playthrough?
community_posts.body: Curious what most people chose for their first run. Did you start on the story-focused mode, the default difficulty, or jump straight into a harder setting? Would you recommend the same choice to new players now that you know how the game ramps up?

community_posts.title: Thoughts on the 2026 performance deep dive?
community_posts.body: The latest performance deep dive for next-gen consoles and high-end PCs had a lot of interesting details about frame-time targets and dynamic resolution. Do you feel encouraged by the roadmap they presented, or are there areas you think they glossed over? Share your platform, settings, and how the game is currently running for you.

community_posts.title: Is Ashen Frontier your new favorite region?
community_posts.body: Now that patch 0.9.5 added the Ashen Frontier, I am curious how people rank it compared to the earlier regions. Do you enjoy the new enemy types and environmental hazards, or do you find them more frustrating than fun? Also, how do you feel about the new co-op challenges tied to this area?

comments.body: I am running a crit-focused bow build and the crafted quiver affixes feel insanely strong in late-game dungeons. Being able to target specific secondary stats through crafting has made a bigger difference than most of my drops.
Same here, but with a melee bleed build. Crafting lets me stack bleed duration and armor shred in a way random drops never do, so I basically live at the forge between runs.

My favorite late-game setup is a support alchemist build that relies on crafted potions and bombs. The consumable recipes from Ashen Frontier give huge burst windows for my group if we coordinate cooldowns.
Crafting is definitely grindy right now, but I like that it gives me a clear goal. I farm specific nodes for an hour, craft a piece, and usually get something meaningfully better than my drops.
For me the timers are the worst part. I would not mind the material grind as much if I could queue up multiple crafts and let them finish while I run dungeons.
I start in the southern Ashen Frontier cliffs, do a loop for ore and leather, then port back to town to reset. That route takes about 12 minutes and fills my bags with most early-game mats.
I can confirm this on my PS5 as well. Crashes happen almost every time I travel from Ashen Frontier back to the capital, especially after long play sessions.
Disabling motion blur and turning off cross-play did not help for me. Hopefully this gets prioritized in the next hotfix, because it makes finishing quests in that region really frustrating.
I get the same hard crashes after a few hours, usually when I open the map in the city hub. It started after patch 0.9.5 and did not happen to me before that.
Interesting, mine tends to happen when I queue back-to-back co-op dungeons. Maybe it is some kind of memory leak that gets worse the more instances you join in one session.
Posting my report here too: digital PS5, performance mode, crashes after about three hours almost every night. Hopefully the devs see the pattern from all these reports.
This happens to me about one in five dungeon queues. I hear the group audio for a second, then I am kicked to the PS5 error screen with a CE-108255-1 message.
Same error code here. It seems more likely when I have cross-play enabled and get matched with a full PC party, so I temporarily turned that off.
I tried rebuilding my PS5 database and reinstalling the game, but the crashes still happen. Glad to see I am not the only one and that this bug is getting visibility.
The hotfix notes mention crash fixes, but this specific dungeon load crash is still happening for me after 0.9.3. I will submit a ticket with my logs as well.
I can reproduce this by standing near the fountain in the main plaza and spamming photo mode on and off. It crashes the game about half the time on my PS5.
Please make sure you look at how daggers scale in late game. Right now they either one-shot trash mobs or feel completely useless on armored elites, with almost no middle ground.
I hope this pass includes clearer in-game explanations of armor penetration and resistances. It is really hard to tell whether my low damage is due to enemy scaling or a bad weapon choice.
Love the idea of smarter bosses as long as telegraphs stay readable. Please avoid making them react too perfectly to player inputs or they will just feel unfair.

Perfect block sounds awesome for high-skill players, but I hope there are accessibility options for those of us with slower reaction times. Maybe a wider timing window on lower difficulties would help.
